questions.q: What does the acronym CSIR stand for?
options: Committee of Science and Innovation Research
Centre for Strategic and Industrial Research
questions.explanation: CSIR stands for the Council of Scientific & Industrial Research, India's premier industrial and applied research organisation.

questions.q: In which year was CSIR established?
questions.explanation: CSIR was established in 1942, during the period of British rule in India.

questions.q: CSIR is best described as which kind of organisation?
options: A network of national research laboratories
questions.explanation: CSIR is a vast network of national laboratories and institutes spread across India focused on industrial and applied science.

questions.q: CSIR primarily focuses on which type of research?
questions.explanation: CSIR's mandate is to drive India's industrial and applied scientific research for national benefit.

questions.q: In which country does CSIR operate?
questions.explanation: CSIR is India's largest publicly funded research and development organisation.

questions.q: CSIR's headquarters is located in which city?
questions.explanation: CSIR is headquartered in New Delhi, the capital of India.

questions.q: Roughly how many decades has CSIR been operating, given it was founded in 1942?
questions.explanation: Founded in 1942, CSIR has been operating for more than eight decades as of the 2020s.

questions.q: CSIR functions under which broad area of the Indian government?
questions.explanation: CSIR operates in the domain of science and technology, under the umbrella of India's Ministry of Science and Technology.

questions.q: Which of these is a well-known CSIR laboratory?
questions.explanation: The National Physical Laboratory (CSIR-NPL) in New Delhi is one of CSIR's flagship laboratories and India's national measurement standards lab.

questions.q: What is the primary aim of CSIR's research network?
options: To advance India's industrial and scientific development
questions.explanation: CSIR was created to drive India's industrial and applied science research and support national development.

questions.q: Who serves as the ex-officio President of CSIR?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister of India is the ex-officio President of CSIR.

questions.q: CSIR is registered under which Indian law?
options: The Reserve Bank of India Act
questions.explanation: CSIR is an autonomous body registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860.

questions.q: Approximately how many national laboratories make up the CSIR network?
questions.explanation: CSIR comprises around 37-38 national laboratories and institutes along with associated outreach and innovation centres.

questions.q: Which CSIR laboratory is famous for genomics and molecular biology research?
options: CSIR-IGIB (Institute of Genomics and Integrative Biology)
CSIR-CGCRI (Central Glass and Ceramic Research Institute)
questions.explanation: CSIR-IGIB in Delhi specialises in genomics and integrative biology, including human genome and disease genetics work.

questions.q: The CSIR-National Aerospace Laboratories (NAL) is located in which city?
questions.explanation: CSIR-NAL, India's leading civil aerospace research lab, is based in Bengaluru.

questions.q: Which CSIR laboratory developed the well-known Saras light transport aircraft programme?
questions.explanation: The Saras civil aircraft was developed by CSIR-National Aerospace Laboratories in Bengaluru.

questions.q: The CSIR-Central Drug Research Institute (CDRI), known for pharmaceutical research, is located in which city?
questions.explanation: CSIR-CDRI, a major drug discovery and development institute, is based in Lucknow.

questions.q: The CSIR-Central Food Technological Research Institute (CFTRI) is located in which city?
questions.explanation: CSIR-CFTRI, focused on food science and technology, is located in Mysuru (Mysore), Karnataka.

questions.q: What is the highest Indian science award administered by CSIR for scientists under 45?
options: Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Prize for Science and Technology
questions.explanation: CSIR administers the Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Prize, India's premier science award for researchers under the age of 45.

questions.q: CSIR-NEERI, which works on pollution control and water treatment, is headquartered in which city?
questions.explanation: The CSIR-National Environmental Engineering Research Institute (NEERI) is headquartered in Nagpur.

questions.q: BGR-34, an Ayurvedic drug developed by CSIR laboratories and launched in 2016, is used to manage which condition?
questions.explanation: BGR-34, a herbal anti-diabetic drug for type 2 diabetes, was developed by the CSIR labs NBRI and CIMAP and commercialised in 2016.

questions.q: CSIR played a key role in India's response to COVID-19 mainly through which kind of contribution?
options: Genome sequencing, diagnostics and drug repurposing research
questions.explanation: CSIR labs such as IGIB and others contributed to COVID-19 genome sequencing, testing kits and drug repurposing efforts.

questions.q: Who was the founding scientist widely regarded as the architect and first Director-General of CSIR?
questions.explanation: Sir Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar was the first Director-General of CSIR and is considered the architect of India's national laboratory system.

questions.q: CSIR-CCMB, a leading molecular and cell biology institute, is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The CSIR-Centre for Cellular and Molecular Biology (CCMB) is located in Hyderabad.

questions.q: The CSIR-Indian Institute of Petroleum (IIP) is located in which city?
questions.explanation: CSIR-IIP, which works on petroleum refining and fuel technology, is based in Dehradun.

questions.q: Which CSIR laboratory is India's custodian of national measurement standards and timekeeping (the national time scale)?
questions.explanation: CSIR-NPL in New Delhi maintains India's national standards of measurement and the Indian Standard Time scale.

questions.q: The CSIR-National Institute of Oceanography (NIO), studying India's seas and oceans, is headquartered in which location?
questions.explanation: CSIR-NIO is headquartered at Dona Paula in Goa, with regional centres along the Indian coast.

questions.q: Which CSIR institute, located in Pune, is known for cutting-edge research in chemistry and chemical sciences?
questions.explanation: The CSIR-National Chemical Laboratory (NCL) in Pune is a premier centre for chemical and materials research in India.

questions.q: CSIR-CIMAP, which researches medicinal and aromatic plants, is headquartered in which city?
questions.explanation: The CSIR-Central Institute of Medicinal and Aromatic Plants (CIMAP) is headquartered in Lucknow.

questions.q: The CSIR-Central Leather Research Institute (CLRI), one of the world's largest leather research bodies, is located in which city?
questions.explanation: CSIR-CLRI is located in Chennai and is among the largest leather research institutes in the world.
